S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Identify the components that every agentic handoff prompt must include
· Translate a vague conversational request into a bounded task brief
· Specify approval gates that pause execution before irreversible actions
· Write verification instructions that tell the agent how to confirm it did the right thing
Design handoffs for readers who are not coders folder workflows, document cleanup, draft generation — without requiring programming knowledge
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: CORE CONCEPTS
blocks: Agentic tools act, not just answer.
The work order becomes a change request.
Scope, authorization, and boundary.
Approval gates.
Verification instructions.

data.title: BEFORE / AFTER PROMPT WALKTHROUGH.
blocks: Scope is explicit folder path, single folder only.
Authorization is explicit rename yes, move no, delete no, new folders no.
The naming rule is specified not "make consistent" but a defined format with a decision rule for missing dates.
· An approval gate is built in before any action.
· Verification is required after completion.

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — THE HUMAN GATE: WHAT YOU MUST

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE: WHAT YOU MUST VERIFY
blocks: Read the proposed action list before approving.
Check the verification output against your expectation.
Check for unintended actions.
Maintain a backup before any handoff involving irreversible operations.
Understand the limits of what you can verify.
data.section: THE HUMAN GATE: WHAT YOU MUST

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Writing a goal instead of a specification.
Omitting the boundary.
Forgetting the prohibition list.
Setting approval gates too late.
Assuming "done" means "correct."
